_metadata.description: Controlled vocabulary for the 3 CAERS adverse-event signal_strength tiers (weak/moderate/strong) in caers_adverse_event_signals.json. UI-bound vocab — carries DISPLAY CONTRACT. Currently the scoring layer is gated off pending PRR/ROR threshold finalization (V1.1 ROADMAP §5.1); vocab is still bundled for future re-enable.
_metadata.purpose: Single source of truth for adverse-event signal-strength labels and (future) penalty weights.
_metadata.alignment: Pipeline source: scripts/data/caers_adverse_event_signals.json `signal_strength` field (159 entries; live: moderate=66, weak=56, strong=37). Penalty wiring: future scoring_config.json caers_signal_penalties (currently disabled).

field_contract.name: User-facing label.
field_contract.threshold_definition: Plain-text description of the PRR/ROR threshold that classifies a signal at this tier (when re-enabled).

_metadata.scoring_status: Disabled at scoring layer per V1.1 ROADMAP §5.1; vocab presentation contract is locked so re-enable doesn't require a coordinated release.

signal_strengths.name: Strong adverse-event signal
signal_strengths.notes: FDA CAERS database shows a strong association between this ingredient and adverse events at population scale. Use with provider awareness.
signal_strengths.threshold_definition: PRR ≥ 4.0 with ≥3 cases reported AND ROR lower 95% CI > 2.0.

signal_strengths.name: Moderate adverse-event signal
signal_strengths.notes: FDA CAERS database shows a moderate association between this ingredient and adverse events. Monitor for the symptoms reported in the signal.

signal_strengths.name: Weak adverse-event signal
signal_strengths.notes: FDA CAERS database shows a weak association — limited cases or marginal statistical signal. Context only; not a strong reason to avoid.
signal_strengths.threshold_definition: PRR 1.5–2.0, or ≥3 cases without sufficient PRR.
